entry.title: Fly Client Proposal
entry.summary:
Tom Harding recently explored the FlyClient concept, a mechanism that facilitates light clients in verifying the entire workload of the heaviest chain using only a ~100KB proof. Originally introduced in a [video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BPNs9EVxWrA&t=8386s), FlyClient has not yet been discussed widely nor has it been incorporated into any Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs). The implementation of this technology could potentially be carried out as a soft fork, similar to the method used for SegWit, by simply incorporating a single hash into the coinbase transaction.
The utilization of FlyClient within Bitcoin's framework suggests a significant advancement in how blockchain lightweight clients operate, offering a more efficient and streamlined method of chain verification. This approach not only reduces data requirements but also enhances the overall accessibility and feasibility of operating light clients. Tom is seeking feedback on this potential integration, highlighting its theoretical ease of implementation and its alignment with existing soft fork strategies.
